producer: hexa verify (bin/hexa-verify, compiled from tool/verify_cli.hexa @ origin/main d73b356d)
channel: p-bar + e+ + e+ -> H-bar + e+  (three-body recombination; the second positron is the third body that carries off the binding energy, dominates radiative recombination in cold trapped plasma)

physics.rate_coefficient_scaling: alpha_3b proportional to e^10 / ( m_e^(1/2) (k_B T)^(9/2) )  =>  alpha_3b ~ n_e * T^(-9/2)
physics.contrast_radiative: radiative recombination p-bar+e+ -> H-bar + gamma scales ~ T^(-1/2) (Kramers) — far weaker T-dependence

closed_form_invariants.density_power: 2 (exact integer: one captured e+ x one spectator e+)

numeric_example.note: ALPHA/ATRAP anchor: cooling positron plasma from 100 K to 4 K boosts the 3-body recombination coefficient by ~1.95e6

verdicts_verbatim: verify --expr recomb_3body_density_power(1)=2  |  calc = 2  == expected 2  |  tier = 🔵 SUPPORTED-FORMAL  (hexa-native closed-form, g_self_verify · TECS-L Tier1)
verify --expr recomb_3body_exponent()=-4.5  |  calc = -4.5  ≈ expected -4.5  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)

verify --expr recomb_3body_tratio(100.0,4.0)=1953125.0  |  calc = 1953125.0  ≈ expected 1953125.0  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)

negative_control.purpose: prove deterministic recompute (not LLM self-judgement) — a wrong claimed value must FALSIFY
cases_verbatim: verify --expr recomb_3body_density_power(1)=3  |  calc = 2  != expected 3  |  tier = 🔴 FALSIFIED  (calc deterministically disagrees — TECS-L result-agnostic closed negative)

verify --expr recomb_3body_exponent()=-4.0  |  calc = -4.5  ≠ expected -4.0  (|Δ|=0.5 > ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🔴 FALSIFIED  (calc deterministically disagrees beyond ε — TECS-L result-agnostic closed negative)

verify --expr recomb_3body_tratio(100.0,4.0)=2000000.0  |  calc = 1953125.0  ≠ expected 2000000.0  (|Δ|=46875.0 > ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🔴 FALSIFIED  (calc deterministically disagrees beyond ε — TECS-L result-agnostic closed negative)

atlas_register: SKIPPED — `hexa atlas register --from-verify` routes through the INSTALLED hexa binary (~/.hx/bin/hexa) whose verify_cli lacks the new fn until a full hx install; ran 🟠 INSUFFICIENT (no calc path in installed binary). register is optional per task. The fns are now MERGED to hexa-lang origin/main (PR #1018, commit 06b61657); reproducible from origin/main verify_cli.hexa + tool/build_hexa_verify.sh, and registerable after the next `hx install`.
hexa_lang_pr: dancinlab/hexa-lang #1018 MERGED (squash, commit 06b61657) — base origin/main; rebased 2x forward-merging sibling fns (#1014 rel_*, #1015 cyclotron_cool_*) per @D d9. #1016 superseded/closed due to concurrent-main conflict.
citations: Mansbach & Keck 1969, Phys. Rev. 181:275 — Monte-Carlo / variational theory of electron-ion three-body recombination in a plasma; T^(-9/2) coefficient scaling.
Glinsky & O'Neil 1991, Phys. Fluids B 3:1279 — guiding-center three-body recombination in a strongly magnetized cold plasma (positron/antiproton regime).
Robicheaux 2006, J. Phys. B 39:3303 — simulations of antihydrogen formation (TBR) in ALPHA/ATRAP nested Penning traps.
Gabrielse et al. 1988 / ATHENA-ATRAP-ALPHA program — cold antihydrogen via three-body recombination of trapped p-bar with cold e+.
constraints_honored: @D g5 — verdict via hexa CLI, verbatim, never self-judged
@D d6 — first-principles closed-form, never fabricated; no calc path => honest 🟠 (not hit here, calc path exists)
@D d9 — committed ONLY explicit own files; siblings' verify_cli.hexa fns (penning_*, pair_threshold_*, transition_factor_1s2s, h1s2s_rydberg) preserved, never swept
demiurge sims = .hexa only — fns authored in tool/verify_cli.hexa, compiled via tool/build_hexa_verify.sh (-lm/-ldl via clang)
